concerns.text: FR-006 requires fitting a 'Parametric 1/sqrt(k) model' as the primary method for threshold detection, with GAM as secondary. The tasks (T029-T031) only implement GAM and a segmented regression fallback. There is no task to implement the primary parametric 1/sqrt(k) model, leaving the spec's primary analytical approach uncovered.

FR-009 requires a sensitivity analysis on the reference value by perturbing it by its SE. While T024 mentions implementing this, it lacks a specific task to aggregate or output the results of this perturbation as a distinct artifact to satisfy SC-006 (measuring robustness). The task risks being an internal check without traceable output.

SC-001 requires measuring the proportion of meta-analyses successfully processed against a target of ≥50. There is no task explicitly dedicated to logging, aggregating, or reporting this success rate metric (e.g., a 'success_rate_report' task) to verify the success criterion is met.

Task T012 conflates the mandatory requirement (FR-001: download real data) with the optional fallback (simulation) in a single task description without a clear conditional branch. This creates ambiguity on whether the 'MVP' phase is complete if only simulation data is generated, potentially violating the spec's priority on real-world data.
